_note: This report overwrites a false prior report. The prior dev agent fabricated completion without modifying the file. Changes below are REAL, verified by grep.

tasks_completed.description: Updated DO NOT rule #7 (line 36) to explicitly reference PUSH_GATE_SID with the three-part fallback chain
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced 'current session' with explicit PUSH_GATE_SID definition. Overwrite guard comparison now uses 'differs from PUSH_GATE_SID'.
tasks_completed.rationale: DO NOT rule #7 had no definition of 'current session' — different subagent invocations used their own ephemeral CLAUDE_SESSION_ID.

tasks_completed.description: Rewrote Phase 10 (~line 398) to define PUSH_GATE_SID, use it for the token session_id field, and use it for the overwrite guard comparison
tasks_completed.changes: Expanded single-sentence Phase 10 prose into 6-step numbered algorithm. Step 1 defines PUSH_GATE_SID with three-part or-chain. Step 4 uses PUSH_GATE_SID for overwrite guard. Step 5 uses PUSH_GATE_SID as session_id in token JSON.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: Phase 10 underspecified — no env var named for session_id. Defining PUSH_GATE_SID once and using it for both write and guard eliminates divergence.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — spec underspecification, not a code regression
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Phase 10 did not specify which env var provides session_id. Second /commit in same user session gets new subagent with different CLAUDE_SESSION_ID. Overwrite guard saw mismatch and skipped write, leaving stale token.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: PUSH_GATE_SID defined once from CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID (stable orchestrator-level) with CLAUDE_SESSION_ID fallback. Both the write and the guard use this single named value.

dev.qa_notes: Verify: (1) PUSH_GATE_SID in file (count>0). (2) Three-part or-chain present at Phase 10. (3) Negative pattern (default= form) absent. (4) DO NOT rule #7 references PUSH_GATE_SID. (5) Line 75 unchanged. (6) hooks/push.sh and scripts/execute-push.py untouched.
